prompts.proposer:
Call proposer_brief() FIRST. The tool output includes pattern_analyst_insights — use trend_champion, seasonality_champion, and insights.recommended_method_hint to bias your candidate selection toward non-mean methods. MUST propose at least 3 candidates including at least 1 non-baseline type. Return ONLY JSON per instructions. IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY reference candidate names that appear in the tool output candidate_library; unknown names will hard-stop.
PATTERN ANALYST INSIGHTS: trend_champion='DWT_svr', seasonality_champion='ARIMA', recommended_method='dba_combination'. Narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.3065) and high seasonality variance (1.1146), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr leads in trend tracking, and early/late horizon specialists differ. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.
prompts.skeptic:
Chame build_debate_packet_tool() PRIMEIRO (inputs via context). Depois retorne APENAS JSON (sem markdown) com add_names, remove_names, params_overrides, rationale, changes, when_good.
IMPORTANT: you MUST ONLY use candidate names from valid_candidate_names; unknown names hard-stop.
You may only remove/override candidates that are in current_candidate_names (or candidates you are adding).

raw.pattern_analyst:
{
  "trend_champion": "DWT_svr",
  "seasonality_champion": "ARIMA",
  "overall_champion": "ARIMA",
  "horizon_specialists": {"early": "ARIMA", "late": "rf"},
  "tier1_models": ["ARIMA", "ETS", "THETA", "DWT_catboost", "ONLY_CWT_rf", "catboost", "NaiveMovingAverage", "CWT_rf"],
  "tier2_models": ["rf", "ONLY_DWT_rf", "CWT_svr", "svr", "ONLY_CWT_catboost", "FT_rf", "NaiveSeasonal", "DWT_rf"],
  "recommended_method_hint": "dba_combination",
  "recommended_weighting_basis": "mixed",
  "key_insights": {
    "rmse_spread_ratio": 1.3065,
    "high_disagreement": true,
    "high_seasonality_variance": true,
    "ytrue_trend_directions": ["up", "up", "down"]
  },
  "cot_narrative": "Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.3065) and high seasonality variance (1.1146), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr leads in trend tracking, and early/late horizon specialists differ. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern."
}
raw.proposer:
{
  "selected_names": ["dba_combination", "best_per_horizon_by_validation", "topk_mean_per_horizon_k3", "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25", "ridge_stacking_l250_topk3"],
  "params_overrides": {"topk_mean_per_horizon_k3": {"top_k": 3}, "inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25": {"top_k": 3, "shrinkage": 0.25}},
  "score_preset": "rmse_focus",
  "force_debate": false,
  "debate_margin": 0.02,
  "rationale": "n_unique_winners=7 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, supporting per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.3065 between best (ARIMA: 3576.28) and worst (DWT_svr: 9417.43) models justifies diverse combination strategies. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=1.1146. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability. Ridge stacking adds non-linear meta-learning. This mix captures trend champions (DWT_svr), seasonality champions (ARIMA/ETS), and leverages model disagreement without overfitting."
}
raw.skeptic:
{
  "add_names": [],
  "remove_names": [],
  "params_overrides": {},
  "rationale": "Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk3), 1 baseline (dba_combination) = 4 different types. No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type). All candidates are in top ranking from candidate_ranking_top. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation has better RMSE (4706.02) but we already have sufficient selection coverage. With n_windows=3, all weighted/selection methods use anti-leakage rolling/expanding strategy. No changes needed.",
  "changes": [],
  "when_good": ""
}

pattern_analyst.cot_narrative: Analysis reveals significant model disagreement (rmse_spread_ratio=1.3065) and high seasonality variance (1.1146), indicating models capture patterns very differently. ARIMA dominates overall RMSE while DWT_svr leads in trend tracking, and early/late horizon specialists differ. These conditions strongly support DBA combination to leverage diverse model behaviors without overfitting to any single pattern.

proposer.rationale: n_unique_winners=7 indicates high horizon heterogeneity, supporting per-horizon selection methods. RMSE spread_ratio=1.3065 between best (ARIMA: 3576.28) and worst (DWT_svr: 9417.43) models justifies diverse combination strategies. Pattern analyst recommends dba_combination for high seasonality_variance=1.1146. With n_windows=3, small top_k=3 with shrinkage=0.25 provides stability. Ridge stacking adds non-linear meta-learning. This mix captures trend champions (DWT_svr), seasonality champions (ARIMA/ETS), and leverages model disagreement without overfitting.

skeptic.rationale: Current candidate set has good diversity: 2 selection (best_per_horizon_by_validation, topk_mean_per_horizon_k3), 1 weighted (inverse_rmse_weights_k3_sh0.25), 1 stacking (ridge_stacking_l250_topk3), 1 baseline (dba_combination) = 4 different types. No redundancy issues (max 2 of any type). All candidates are in top ranking from candidate_ranking_top. Universe leaderboards show best_single_by_validation has better RMSE (4706.02) but we already have sufficient selection coverage. With n_windows=3, all weighted/selection methods use anti-leakage rolling/expanding strategy. No changes needed.
